questions.question_text: A store sells notebooks for $3 each and pens for $1.50 each. Jasmine buys a total of 10 items and spends exactly $24.00. How many notebooks did she buy?
questions.explanation: Let n = number of notebooks and p = number of pens. Set up the system: n + p = 10 and 3n + 1.5p = 24. From the first equation, p = 10 − n. Substitute into the second: 3n + 1.5(10 − n) = 24, which gives 3n + 15 − 1.5n = 24, so 1.5n = 9, and n = 6. Jasmine bought 6 notebooks (and 4 pens). Check: 6($3) + 4($1.50) = $18 + $6 = $24. ✓

questions.topic: Geometry — Right Triangles and the Pythagorean Theorem
questions.question_text: In the figure below, triangle ABC has a right angle at B. The length of AB is 8 units and the length of BC is 6 units. What is the length of AC, in units?
questions.explanation: Because the right angle is at B, the hypotenuse is AC. By the Pythagorean theorem: AC² = AB² + BC² = 8² + 6² = 64 + 36 = 100. Therefore AC = √100 = 10. This is the classic 6-8-10 right triangle (a scaled 3-4-5 triple).

visual_json.description: Right triangle ABC with the right angle at B. Vertices: A at the origin, B directly to the right of A, C directly above B.

questions.question_text: If sin(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, what is the value of sin(2θ)?
questions.explanation: Use the double-angle identity: sin(2θ) = 2 sin(θ) cos(θ). We are given sin(θ) = 3/5. Because θ is in the first quadrant, cos(θ) is positive. Using the Pythagorean identity, cos²(θ) = 1 − sin²(θ) = 1 − 9/25 = 16/25, so cos(θ) = 4/5. Therefore sin(2θ) = 2 · (3/5) · (4/5) = 24/25.
